title: Responsible AI in Production
concept: Responsible AI deployment requires maintaining model cards, implementing human-in-the-loop validation, and defining ethical boundaries.

exercises.prompt: Recap: How do deepfake detectors recognize AI-generated faces?

options.text: By finding pixel artifacts, weird pupil reflections, and motion inconsistencies
By checking if the file is saved in ONNX format
By counting the total number of channels

exercises.explain: Synthesis models leave subtle signatures, such as mismatched lighting and unnatural eye movements, that detectors can flag.

cards.title: The ethical launch gate
cards.body: You've built a model that is fast, quantized, robust to attacks, and highly accurate. But should you launch it? Deploying AI responsibly means looking beyond technical performance to consider the human impact. Let's look at the launch checklist!

cards.title: Responsible AI Practices
cards.body: Responsible AI is a framework for safe deployment. It requires publishing model cards (documenting performance limits and biases), establishing human-in-the-loop verification for high-risk decisions, and defining clear ethical boundaries.

cards.title: A warning label on medicine
cards.body: A pharmacy doesn't sell a drug with a simple label saying "it works". They provide a detailed pamphlet outlining side effects, correct dosages, and warnings (model cards). For high-risk prescriptions, a doctor must sign off (human-in-the-loop).

cards.title: Model Cards in practice
cards.body: A company launches a crowd-counting model. Their model card documents: 'Accurate down to 50 lux of light; performance degrades by 30% in heavy rain; do not use for individual tracking or surveillance'.

cards.title: Safety is an ongoing process
cards.body: You might think ethical AI is a one-time approval before launch. In reality, as models encounter new user demographics and camera hardware in the wild, you must continuously monitor for performance drifts and newly emerging biases.

cards.title: Responsible AI Checklist
cards.body: Key pillars of ethical model deployment:

left.text:
Documents data sources
Discloses accuracy limits
Highlights known biases
Specifies intended use cases

right.text:
AI flags risky cases
Humans make final decisions
Prevents automated failures
Protects high-risk domains

exercises.prompt: What is a model card in machine learning production?

options.text: A document disclosing a model's performance, data sources, biases, and intended use cases
A physical graphic card used to train neural networks
An export format compatible with Apple Neural Engine
A tool that fuses Conv and ReLU layers

exercises.explain: Model cards promote transparency by documenting how a model was built and its known limitations.

exercises.prompt: True or False: In high-risk domains like medical diagnosis or legal systems, AI models should make final automated decisions without human oversight.
exercises.explain: False. High-risk systems should use human-in-the-loop structures to prevent catastrophic automated errors.

exercises.sentence: Integrating human review for uncertain AI classifications is called human-in-the-___.
exercises.explain: Human-in-the-loop keeps experts involved in verifying critical or low-confidence model decisions.

exercises.prompt: Which of these is an example of an ethical boundary for vision AI?

options.text: Refusing to deploy facial recognition systems for mass surveillance
Limiting the training dataset size to save cloud server storage
Only using ResNet-50 backbones to save GPU memory

exercises.explain: Setting ethical boundaries means deciding not to build or deploy AI systems for harmful or invasive purposes.

exercises.prompt: You are deploying an automated security screening system at an airport. The model scans bags and flags potential threats. How do you deploy this system responsibly?

options.text: Deploy the system with a human-in-the-loop: when the AI flags a bag, a security officer manually inspects it; publish a model card detailing performance limits.
Allow the AI to automatically confiscate bags without notifying security guards.
Keep the system's data sources and failure rates completely secret to avoid bad publicity.
Run the AI on raw, unencrypted public cloud networks.

exercises.explain: Next up: Computer Vision Capstone. For high-risk screening, combining human-in-the-loop validation with transparent model card documentation ensures safety, accountability, and ethical compliance.
